ACCU1_after_load_input.interpretation: PowerMeter_KwH input has already overflowed before this network ran — likely a faulty meter reading or a sensor wraparound.

STATUS_word.note: OV=0 here because IEEE 754 arithmetic on +Inf does NOT set the overflow flag — the result is a valid IEEE 754 special value. This is a R1 false-confidence trap: AN OV would not catch this.

rule_violations: R1 (overflow protection) — the network has T #Diff_kWh but no `AN OV; SAVE; CLR; A BR` follow-up. Even with the pattern, OV would not have caught this case because IEEE 754 silently produces +Inf rather than setting OV.
Stronger guard needed: explicit `==R` against +/-Inf, or upstream sanity-bound check on the input PowerMeter_KwH before subtraction.
expected_diagnosis.verdict: Abnormal. The R1 pattern alone is insufficient when the upstream input is already +Inf. Need stronger validation upstream OR an explicit `JUO` (jump-unordered) check after the arithmetic to catch IEEE 754 special values.
next_steps: Route to awl-step7 Analysis Mode for the source FB: identify where PowerMeter_KwH is loaded from the meter and whether its raw analog value has range validation.
Recommend an improvement: range-bound check on the input (e.g., if PowerMeter_KwH > 1.0e9 then suspect overflow → fault).
Recommend adding `JUO Skip;` after each REAL arithmetic when input may be unbounded.
